podcasts.name: The Ecom Academy
podcasts.title: Scale Shopify to $1m (Step by Step)
podcasts.transcript: In this video, I want to take you through the exact system that I use to scale e-commerce brands to a million dollars plus per year. It's the same system that I've used on my stores. One that I just scaled Fike Direct to $1.18 million in under 18 months. It's the same system that I'm now using on my current brand, Dose, that we're scaling using AI, where AI does 80% of the work and humans only do 20%. It's the same system that we teach inside the e-commerce academy. It's helped us scale hundreds and hundreds of brands. In this video, I'm going to take you through the three parts of that system. I'm going to show you how we get more traffic. I'm going to show you how to convert that traffic into sales. And then I'm going to show you how to scale that so it compounds and gets you to that million dollar a year lifestyle business. Now, I'm not going to teach you how to build uh enterprise level brand where you need lots of staff and lots of people and lots of investment. I'm going to show you how to build a great business that gives you a great income and even better, it gives you a great lifestyle. So, let's get into it. Now, just before we do that, if at any time during this video you just want to reach out to me and ask me some questions, I'll put my Instagram down below. And what I'll get you to do is just DM me the word say um say scale so I know you've watched this video and then we can jam on how I can help you grow your store. Growing an e-commerce store is a little bit like climbing a ladder. We will start at the bottom and we climb each rung of the ladder and it's a different stage and it's usually a different revenue stage and I call this the uh the stages of scale. Okay. Now, the interesting thing about e-commerce is the stages of scale get easier the higher you go up the ladder, right? So, the first few stages are usually a lot of work. They're really hard. There's a lot of effort. There's a lot of time. But as you climb the ladder, each of those stages gets easier and easier. And the mistake that a lot of people make is they give up when they're on the bottom two or three rungs and they're like, you know what, I can't climb to the next step and I can't get to the next step. But if they do go past that step, then it all becomes a lot more easy to get there, right? and then I'm going to give you an insight into the system that helps you get through those stages. Okay. So, stage number one is a revenue gate of around 0 to $10,000 a month mark. And that's where we see most stores start out. And this is typically the side hustle stage. This is where we're finding out our product market fit. This is where we're learning how to import products, how to develop products, how to market, how to build stores. There's a lot in this part, right? We're learning how to create ads. We're learning how to send emails all in this side hustle. and we're only making 0 to $10,000, right? And some of us don't even get to $10,000 because it becomes a lot of work. But what we find is the person that pushes through past that side hustle phase moves into the next phase, which is the 10 to 30k month mark. We start to see some gains. Okay? And this is where we go from side hustle into full-time. We're making just enough income to replace our full-time job, and then this can become our full-time job. Now, by the time we get here, we've we've learned how to build a store. We've learned how to sort of do marketing. We're doing a little bit of emailing. We're doing enough to get some consistent income. Okay. So, we're starting to see the uh the fruits of our labor, but that's where a lot of people just sort of plateau and they get stuck where they really want to get to, which is the next phase. This is where we really see the sweet spot from people going full-time into this next part. And that is the $30,000 a month to the $60,000 a month, right? And this is where we we call this the stability phase. Okay? And a lot of the people in the brands that we work with do come to us at this phase here where they've hit full-time. They know they've got a business, but they just don't know how to get to the level. So our job is how do we get them to stability and then how do we get them to the next phase? And the next phase is where things really kick off. And that is where they go from this $60,000 to $100,000 a month mark. And this is where the business actually starts to be able to scale. And then they can start to see what all this hard work was about. And like I said earlier, the part that is really hard is actually here. This is where all the hard work is done. So if we put in the effort here and we learn the systems and strategies here, as we get into this part here, this is where we start to see that the business becomes a little bit easier and it works for us. And by putting in the right systems and understanding what it is to grow, we're going to be able to move into the next phase. And the next phase is where we get to our 100K to 300K. And this is where we really have a lifestyle business. That is where we've got a million dollars a year coming in. We can live life on our own terms. We've got a team working for us. We've got AI agents working for us and we can really start to enjoy life again. This isn't a big team. This isn't a huge enterprise. This is just a nice business that brings us in 100 grand a month and it's working for us, right? And it's a great little business. But there's some of us and some of the teams that were some of the businesses that we work with, they want to go to that final level. And this is where we really start to build an enterprise business. And this is where we get to that 300k per month mark. And this is where we start to build a freedom business. Okay, these are all the stages. Now, a lot of the brands we work with operate at many different stages here. We have a lot of people sitting in this phase. We have people who are moving in this phase and we even have some in this phase. And every business is different. And that's why we want to make sure we've got a system that plays into the stages of scale that you want to operate in. So, what I want you to do is think about this stage right here. and identify where do you want to get your business to? Maybe you're at side hustle and you want to just get to full-time. Maybe you're at full-time and you want to become more stable. Maybe you're at stability and you just want to get into this scale phase. Where is it you want your business to be? Because this is what's going to determine the strategy. Because if we don't know where we want to get to, we can't put a road map in place to get us there. And I want to take you through what that road map is now so that you can identify what it is you need to do in your business to hit the stage that you want to hit. Now, the system we use to grow stores is called the e-commerce operating system, and it's a framework that I've designed over 15 years, and I've been fortunate enough to work inside some pretty big brands, some which I've scaled to over hundred million dollars a year. So, what I've done is I've taken the strategies I've learned in the big brands, and I've taken the strategies I've applied in my own brands in an easy to apply roadmap that you guys can implement. And I'll take you through step by step what each of those are. Now, I'll break it down into the pillars, and I'll break it down into the strategies so you know exactly what you need to do. The whole purpose of the operating system is to basically grow brands to a million dollars a year as a starting point. Once you hit a million a year, we're then going to move into a million dollars a month. The great thing about the way we've designed this system is no matter what size your store is, whether you're just starting out or whether you're already at a million dollars a year, this still applies because it is one of those compounding, ever growing systems that basically helps itself. The way we do it is we break it up into three core pillars that grow an e-commerce business. And they are traffic, conversions, and scale. So, we can't have an e-commerce business if we don't have traffic coming in. And we can't have an e-commerce business if our traffic isn't converting. And then we can't grow if our store isn't profitable. We can't scale. So what our goal together is is where do we get our traffic from? How do we make sure that that traffic is converting? And then how do we do that on autopilot so this system compounds? I'm going to take you through those strategies that you can implement right now. So let's get started with traffic. There are three types of traffic that we think about when scaling an e-commerce store. The first one everybody knows about and that is paid traffic. that is where we buy ads and we pay Meta or we pay Google and it sends us traffic. The good thing about this is we can get traffic basically. Now the bad thing about it is it costs us money. Okay, not necessarily a bad thing but we still have to pay for it, right? But if we want it now we have to pay for it. That's the way it works. Now the next traffic type is content. Basically the internet is just content, right? We put content up on Instagram. We put content out to Google. We create websites. People are just consuming content. So what we want to do is we want to create content that people watch and then they visit our site because they've watched it. Okay? Now the great thing about content is it is an asset that we create that we can own. Okay? So it's something that we can own and we can use right forever and ever and ever. The other great thing about content is it's a way to nurture people that come in from paid ads. So if someone come in from paid ads and then they see our content, if they're not buying from us, at least they're starting to get to know us and we're warming them up. So content is a great warming piece of traffic. The third type of traffic that we can grab is what we call earn traffic. And by the way, I apologize about my writing. Hopefully you can hear and watch at the same time. But earn traffic is where we earn the right for someone who has traffic and they send it to us, right? So this could be someone like Google who has lots of search traffic. It could be an AI engine. It could be a referral partner. It could be an influencer. But the idea is that we build up such a reputation and we're known for our products is that people say, "Hey, if you need this product, go to those people." Okay. Now, the great thing about earned is it's free, but the bad thing is is it's slow. Okay. It takes a bit of time to earn a reputation. It's not something you can just go out of the gates and have someone send traffic for you. But the cool thing is if someone sees us through earn and then they start seeing our content, we can start to see that this middle part becomes an asset for us. Okay. Now, what we're going to do is I'm going to take you through the strategies in each of these that you can implement in your business that we implement to grow stores. The first one in paid is we only look at two different paid channels. We either look at meta or we look at Google. And the reason we do only those two is they both serve different purposes. Meta is what we call an awareness channel. It is where we put our brands and products in front of people who are not actively searching for them, but we might be able to get their interest. And it's usually great for like a visual product that's something that is is new or it looks great or it's appealing visually because usually in meta we use videos or photos to get people's attention. And if people aren't actively searching for it, we just want to try and grab their attention. The second one which is Google is what we call a capture channel. And what that means is if someone is actively searching for your product, you want to be advertising where they're searching for it. Okay? So let's just say you have you sell black hats and someone goes searches for black hats. it's much better to be advertising then. So, when we're first starting out, we only want to choose one of these channels to get started. And we say we choose one as a primary channel and then one as a secondary channel. So, if you're under $30,000 a month, you probably shouldn't be choosing both of these channels cuz it's a lot of money and a lot of work. Just choose the one that is right for you. Is it are you creating awareness or are you capturing demand? Have a think about that. Okay. The second pillar when it comes to content and when we're nurturing people is we think about email and we think about social media. So these are places where we own the audience and we can create content for that audience. We can send them emails, we can create reals, we can do social media posts and what we're doing is we are showing people that we understand who they are and we are building a community around that. Okay. Now, when it comes to emails, we're not just doing flows or automations. We're also doing campaigns. And we recommend sending at least two emails per week. And we implement this inside our e-commerce academy where we've got most members sending emails two to three times a week. And every email makes them money because they write the right emails. And it's basically a recipe to print money. The second one is social media is creating content that gets views. And it's making sure that we get as many eyeballs as possible. We're not just creating content for people that follow us. We're going to be creating content for people that might be interested in us. And the way the algorithm works now is if we create great content, it's going to go out and find us new customers that will then follow us and then visit our site. Now, if we move on to the third pillar, that is the earn pillar. The two areas we focus on here is SEO and AIO. So, getting found in search engines and also getting found in AI answers. And then the last one is referrals and that is getting other brands to send us traffic. Again, I apologize for my writing. Right? So that is like getting uh a brand that is a similar audience to but not might not sell your product. They might refer business to you. SEO and AIO is making sure that we optimize our website in such a way so they get found in those search and answer engines. Okay. Now that we know all these strategies, we now need to identify which one should we work on for our business because every business is a little bit different. But I don't want you working on all of these because that would become overwhelming. We just want to work on the ones that matter. Okay. So if you were to choose one from each of these pillars, which ones would they be? So are you working in meta? Is that something we really need to focus on? If we are, don't worry about Google. If we're thinking about content, are we just going to do email and not worry about social? Right? Just do one thing at a time. And if we're going in the earn, are we going to just do referrals and not worry about SEO? Now, it's only you that can say that what works for you. Or you can work with one of our coaches and we'll tell you which one it is. But I want you to only identify one in each of these pillars and only work on those. Don't get distracted by other tactics that don't matter just now. Work on the ones that matter. Think about if I was to do this thing and I was to do it right, which would have the best outcome in the fastest amount of time in my business. And that's how we think about this system. And then what we do is we then sprinkle a little bit of AI magic on top. And that's where we go, okay, so if meta is the is the engine, then what AI implementation do we need to do here? What AI systems can we install from email? Do we have an email system that we can put in place? Can we use AI to do it? Right? And all of these things can now be amplified with AI. And let's just say we're doing referrals. Can we do outreach using AI? Okay. So, we identify what we need to work on in the pillar and then we understand what we need to do and then we use AI to help us. Okay. AI does the 80% of the grunt work. The human does the 20%. Let's now move on to the conversion side of things. Now there's also three areas in conversions and the first one is dialing in your offer. Now the offer is really really important and when I say offer I don't mean uh like having a discount or having a promotion. What I mean is how are we presenting your product in such a way that it's an irresistible offer that someone looks at the product and says oh my god I need that. Hell yeah I'm going to buy it. That's the first part because if we don't get the offer right then everything gets really hard. If the offer is great then marketing gets easy. everything gets easy. So, it's important to dial that offer in. The second thing we need to do is we need to implement optimization ongoing. Sorry about my writing again, but we want to make sure that in order to grow our business that we're getting 1% better every day. We're continually optimizing our business so that we're looking at the numbers, we know what to change, and we've got systems in place that are helping us do that. And then the third part of conversions is something that most people don't realize has the biggest impact on conversions and that is having a great support system. Okay, if you think about it, you've got a great offer and a great site, but there's no help. There's no refunds and exchanges. There's no one to ask questions. People aren't going to trust you or buy from you. So support's really, really important. All right, so let me take you through the tactics of conversions that you can implement. From an offer perspective, we want to make sure that our offer is really well articulated and presented across our PDP, which stands for product detail page. And we also want to make sure that we're building out landing pages for our ads. So, the offer needs to have five signals on there. And there's specific signals that I talk about in another that I talk about in another video. So, go look for that. But, um, those five signals need to be on the page so that people will buy from you. Okay? So we have a system which we call a perfect product page that we install and that's how we can turn the offer into a great PDP. The other thing we need to think about on offer is value. And value means that when someone comes to buy from you, how do we increase the amount they spend? So rather than them just spending say $50, how do we make them spend 75? How do we make them spend 100? Because if you're using paid ads, we want to make sure that we get as much money out of that ad spend as humanly possible. We don't need to actually change anything in our ads if we're getting people to spend more at the other end, right? We'll instantly double our rows if we double the amount someone spends with us. So really important to get that value right. When it comes to optimization, the two areas we look at is do we have the right themes in place? And do we have the right apps? Does the store do what we want it to do? Is it easy for the customer to buy things from us? Is it easy for them to upsell? Is it easy for them to do subscriptions? Do we have the right apps? And is the theme usable? If we get that right, then we can implement a system which we call CRO. And this usually only happens when stores are getting to that $60 to $100,000 a month mark. We usually don't talk about it in in the lower stores because we need to get our stability right before we move into scale. But CRO stands for conversion rate optimization. And that is making sure we're continually pulling and tweaking and analyzing so that we make our store better every time. We don't tell startup stores to do CRO because they can't because you need the volume to do it. All right? But CRO is super super important when getting to that million dollar mark. And then when we go to support, the two areas we focus on and the two strategies you need to really implement is do we have a great customer experience or customer service system. And the reason this is important is money follows speed, right? We want to make sure that if someone has a question, they get it answered really fast or they can find the answer to a question easily. They want to know that if they send you a message on Instagram that you're going to respond to them or if they send you a message on email, you respond to them. Now, that's a challenge because as a brand owner building a lifestyle business, you don't want to be on your phone every day answering questions. This is where we can use AI to help us with that. Remember, money follows speed and people buy from people. So, they want to make sure there's always someone there to help you out. Now, the final part of conversions and the support section is getting feedback. And this is in the form of say product reviews. It's in the form of comments on posts. It's in the form of surveys that we might do. But feedback helps improve your conversions because feedback tells us the things that people don't like and the things that people do like. And if we can amplify the things that people do like and if we can try and improve the things that they don't like, then our conversions will increase. Amazon once did a study where they tested listings on Amazon with product reviews without product reviews and they found that the conversion rate increased just having product reviews increased by like double literally overnight. So, we say to everyone, make sure you put product reviews on your store. It's a great way of getting feedback, but it's also a great way of increasing your conversion rate. Okay. We need to understand, you know, how much our stock costs us, how much we can spend on ads, all those sorts of things. If we don't know our economics, then we're just absolutely flying blind.
